title: Mysore Pak

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Ghee (Clarified Butter) – 1 cup (melted, plus extra for greasing)

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare the Sugar Syrup
steps: In a thick-bottomed pan, combine sugar and water.
Heat gently and stir until sugar dissolves completely.
Bring to a single-thread consistency (drop a bit of syrup between your thumb and forefinger; it should form a single thread).
Tip: Avoid overcooking; too thick syrup makes Mysore Pak hard.

instruction.title: Step 2: Roast the Gram Flour (Besan)
steps: Dry roast the besan lightly on low heat in a separate pan for 2–3 minutes.
Ensure it is aromatic but not browned.
This removes raw flavor and ensures smooth texture.

instruction.title: Step 3: Combine and Cook
steps: Reduce sugar syrup to a gentle simmer.
Slowly add roasted besan to the syrup in small portions, stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Once incorporated, start adding melted ghee gradually, 1–2 tbsp at a time, while stirring constantly.
Cook on low heat, stirring continuously.
After a few minutes, the mixture will start to leave the sides of the pan and form bubbles—this is the perfect moment.

instruction.title: Step 4: Set the Mysore Pak
steps: Grease a tray or plate with ghee.
Pour the hot mixture immediately and spread evenly.
Let it rest for 5 minutes; while slightly warm, cut into desired shapes (squares or diamonds).
Allow it to cool completely; it will firm up with a slightly porous, melt-in-mouth texture.

chefTips: Ghee Quality: Use high-quality, aromatic ghee; it’s the main flavor.
Low Heat Cooking: Crucial to avoid burning; slow and steady gives the soft texture.
Consistency Check: The right point is when the mixture leaves the sides and makes a spongy sound when stirred.
Storage: Keep in an airtight container for up to 7–10 days; it stays soft longer if stored in a cool, dry place.
